title: Session Lifecycle and Cache Management

create_session: SessionService → Database: INSERT INTO sessions (uuid, user_id, meta, last_activity, created_at)
SessionService → Redis: SET session-cache:{uuid} JSON EX 300
getSession: alt [cache hit] Redis → SessionService: JSON string → parse → return session
alt [cache miss] SessionService → Database: SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE uuid = ? LIMIT 1
SessionService → Database: get_user({ id: session.user_id }) [for user_uid]
SessionService → Redis: SET session-cache:{uuid} JSON EX 300
SessionService → Caller: return session (internal values stripped)
cacheSession: SessionService → Redis: SET session-cache:{uuid} JSON EX 300
getCachedSession: alt [parse error / uuid mismatch] → invalidateCachedSession(uuid) → return null
remove_session: SessionService → Database: SELECT user_id FROM sessions WHERE uuid = ? LIMIT 1
SessionService → Database: DELETE FROM sessions WHERE uuid = ?
get_user_sessions: if session invalid or user_id mismatch → Redis: SREM user-sessions-set {uuid}  [skip]
SessionService → Caller: return sessions (internal values stripped)
flush_loop_updateSessions: SessionService → Redis: SET flush-lock:{uuid} 1 EX 30 NX  [acquire lock]
if session missing or not flushPending → Redis: SREM flush-pending {uuid}  [skip]
SessionService → Database: UPDATE sessions SET meta=?, last_activity=? WHERE uuid=? AND last_activity < ?
if no rows affected → Database: SELECT uuid to check existence → if gone: invalidateCachedSession
after loop: for each updated user_id:
SessionService → Database: UPDATE user SET last_activity_ts=? WHERE id=?
SessionService → Redis: GET UserRedisCacheSpace key for user
if cached user outdated → UserRedisCacheSpace: setUser(updatedUser)

key_relationships: SessionService depends_on UserRedisCacheSpace (for user cache invalidation during flush)
SessionService depends_on get_user helper (for user_uid resolution on DB fallback)
important_notes: Cache TTL is 5 minutes (300s). Sessions expire from Redis automatically.
Flush loop runs every 5–60 seconds (randomised) to batch-write activity timestamps to DB.
Distributed lock (NX flag, 30s TTL) prevents concurrent flush of the same session.
getSession always marks session as flushPending to trigger a DB write on the next flush cycle.
create_session writes to DB first, then caches (flushState 'unchanged', no flush pending).
remove_session reads both Redis and DB to obtain user_id before full invalidation.
get_user_sessions is fully Redis-backed; stale UUIDs are cleaned up on read.
getCachedSession validates uuid field in parsed JSON to prevent cache poisoning.
